profile.name: Daniel
profile.role_in_household: Primary earner; business owner; tends to lead financial discussions and keep the big picture in mind.
profile.financial_literacy_level: High (practical): comfortable discussing brokerage accounts, retirement accounts, and cash flow; tends to remember specific figures and asks for tax-aware framing.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate but protection-leaning: willing to invest, yet frequently steers back to capital preservation and downside scenarios; prefers guardrails (buffers, diversification) over aggressive growth.
profile.communication_style: Direct and numbers-forward; will quote or correct amounts, then pause to confirm. Uses occasional hedges like "roughly" or "if I’m remembering right" when discussing items he doesn’t track day-to-day. Asks pointed follow-ups about taxes and account types.
profile.decision_making_style: Analytical and comparative: wants options laid out with trade-offs, then decides after a short reflection; prefers to be the final signer but values spouse buy-in.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open: willing to share details and listen, but expects competence and clear explanations before delegating.
hidden_concerns: whether retirement timing is realistic given multiple income sources and taxes
whether estate planning goals are adequately covered without overcomplicating investments
whether insurance costs are truly justified long-term

profile.name: Emily
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; employed in education; focuses on family priorities and long-term security, often deferring on technical investment details.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate (conceptual): understands budgeting and retirement goals, but can mix up account labels (e.g., taxable vs. retirement) and may ask for plain-English definitions and summaries.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate-to-conservative in practice: comfortable with investing when it’s explained as a plan, but becomes cautious when hearing about concentrated positions or alternatives; prefers steady progress and clarity over optimization.
profile.communication_style: Warm, collaborative, and story-based; speaks in round numbers and approximations ("around," "give or take"). Uses clarifying questions and occasional self-corrections ("wait—premium versus payout?"). Will interrupt gently if something feels unclear or too jargon-heavy.
emotional_tendencies: stress when conversations get too technical
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-driven: wants to understand the “why,” then aligns with Daniel if the plan feels safe and family-centered; prefers a clear next-step checklist.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Moderate and relationship-based: trust increases with patience, transparency, and being heard; skeptical of salesy tones.
internal_biases: status quo bias (prefers not to change too many things at once)
hidden_concerns: ensuring the children’s needs and future support are protected without taking on debt
fear of making a mistake with taxes or account withdrawals
worry that retirement planning could reduce flexibility for family expenses
